---
title: "Platelet Ca¬≤‚Āļ signalling ‚ÄĒ pathway, equation & rate reference (v0.7)"
subtitle: "A walk-through of the model figure: every pathway, its governing equations and rate constants"
author: "Steve Haigh"
date: 2026-06-24
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    geometry: "margin=2.5cm"
    fontsize: 11pt
    mainfont: "STIX Two Text"
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: blue
    urlcolor: blue
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    include-in-header: ../pandoc-header.tex
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
---

## Purpose and scope

This is a **reference document** for the v0.7 platelet calcium-signalling
pathway diagram (@fig-pathway). It walks the figure component by component and,
for each, states (i) the biological role, (ii) the governing equation(s) **exactly
as implemented**, (iii) the rate constants with their numeric values, units and
source, and (iv) which model state variable(s) the term touches and whether it
feeds back into the calcium ODE or is a terminal output.

Every equation and constant below is taken directly from the model source. The
two homes are:

- **The calcium ODE** ‚ÄĒ `reconstruction/platelet/dataclasses/process/calcium_signalling.py`,
  whose pure right-hand side `_ode_rhs(t, y, t_sim_start, config, step_inputs)`
  integrates a 58-species state vector each 1 s outer step. Rate constants are
  loaded from `reports/params/calcium-v0.6.toml` into the `_KINETICS` dict.
- **Three downstream processes** ‚ÄĒ `granule_secretion.py`,
  `thromboxane_synthesis.py`, `integrin_activation.py` (in
  `models/platelet/processes/` with parameter dataclasses under
  `reconstruction/platelet/dataclasses/process/`). These read ODE states and,
  in two cases, feed an autocrine signal back into the ODE.

Line numbers cite the source as of git commit `4fe847b9`. Where a constant is a
calibration/model choice rather than a measured value, that is stated ‚ÄĒ most
receptor copy numbers are measured, most rate constants are calibrated.

![**The v0.7 platelet Ca¬≤‚Āļ signalling network.** Plasma-membrane receptors split into a **Gq/P2Y1 arm** (green) and a **Gi/P2Y12 arm** (purple) feeding the cytosolic Ca¬≤‚Āļ machinery; **PKC** is the activation hub, driving three terminal outputs. The components are walked through immediately below, and in full in the sections that follow.](../figures/v0.7-platelet-ca-pathway.png){#fig-pathway width=100%}

### Reading the diagram

Stepping through @fig-pathway:

- **Gq / P2Y1 arm (green).** Agonists ADP (P2Y1), thrombin (PAR1, PAR4) and
  autocrine TXA‚āā (TP) ‚Üí **GőĪq** ‚Üí **PLCő≤** ‚Üí PIP‚āā ‚Üí **IP‚āÉ + DAG** ‚Üí **IP‚āÉR**-mediated
  Ca¬≤‚Āļ release from the dense tubular system (DTS). *(‚Üí ¬ßA‚ÄďE.1)*
- **Gi / P2Y12 arm (purple).** ADP on **P2Y12** ‚Üí **GőĪi** ‚Üí ‚Üďadenylyl cyclase ‚Üí
  ‚Üď**cAMP** ‚Üí ‚Üď**PKA**, relieving the PKA brake on IP‚āÉR, integrin and secretion.
  VASP-phosphorylation (VASP-P) is the clinical readout; **clopidogrel** blocks
  P2Y12. *(‚Üí ¬ßA.5)*
- **Direct Ca¬≤‚Āļ entry.** The ionotropic **P2X1** channel (top-left, ATP-gated)
  admits Ca¬≤‚Āļ directly ‚ÄĒ no G protein, so no cascade arrow leaves it. *(‚Üí ¬ßA.2)*
- **Cytosolic Ca¬≤‚Āļ handling.** **SERCA** refills the DTS; **PMCA** and **NCX**
  extrude to the outside; **STIM1/Orai1** give store-operated entry (SOCE);
  mitochondrial **MCU/NCLX** exchange buffers the transient. *(‚Üí ¬ßE‚ÄďG.1)*
- **Buffers.** DTS luminal proteins (CALR, HSP90B1, BiP, CREC) and cytosolic
  **CaM** buffer free Ca¬≤‚Āļ. *(‚Üí ¬ßG.2)*
- **PKC hub & outputs.** DAG + Ca¬≤‚Āļ ‚Üí **PKC (őĪ/ő≤/őī)**, which brakes the Gq cascade
  (P2Y1 desensitisation, PLCő≤ phosphorylation) and drives three **terminal
  outputs**: granule release (dense őī ‚ÄĒ Ca¬≤‚Āļ/ADP/5-HT, incl. the autocrine ADP
  loop; őĪ ‚ÄĒ fibrinogen/vWF/P-selectin surface exposure), thromboxane synthesis
  (COX-1; **aspirin** target), and őĪIIbő≤3 inside-out activation (the PAC-1
  flow-cytometry readout, driven via PI3K/Akt ‚Üí Rap1b). *(‚Üí ¬ßD, G.3‚ÄďG.5)*
- **Optional drug targets (dashed in the figure).** clopidogrel (P2Y12),
  aspirin (COX-1).

---

## Model architecture & conventions

**Integration.** The ODE is integrated with
`scipy.integrate.solve_ivp(..., method='BDF', atol=1e-3, rtol=1e-6, max_step=dt)`
over a 1 s outer step (`calcium_signalling.py:1535‚Äď1542`). State is carried as
integer-equivalent **counts**; each rate law converts to ¬ĶM internally.

**Compartment volumes & unit conversion** (lines 61‚Äď80):

| Symbol | Value | Meaning | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| `V_CYT_L` | $6.0\times10^{-15}$ L | cytosol | Purvis 2008 |
| `V_DTS_L` | $0.258\times10^{-15}$ L | DTS (4.3 % of cytosol) | Purvis 2008 |
| `V_EX_L` | $6.64\times10^{-14}$ L | pericellular (~66 fL) | autocrine calibration |
| `N_A` | $6.022\times10^{23}$ | Avogadro | ‚ÄĒ |

$$\text{conc}_{\mu M} = \frac{\text{count}}{N_A\, V_L\, 10^{-6}}\quad(\texttt{\_UM\_PER\_COUNT\_CYT/DTS/EX})$$

**Physical constants** (lines 215‚Äď234): $F = 96485$ C/mol, $R = 8.314$ J/mol¬∑K,
$T = 310$ K, giving $RT/zF \approx 0.01334$ V for $z=2$. Membrane potentials:
$V_{IM} = 0$ V (DTS membrane), $V_{PM} = -0.060$ V (plasma membrane, Dolan 2014).
Electrodiffusive channel fluxes (P2X1, IP3R, SOCE) use a Nernst driving force
$J = -\gamma_s\,N\,P_o\,(V_m - E_{Ca})\,N_A/zF$ with
$E_{Ca} = (RT/zF)\ln([\mathrm{Ca}]_\text{out}/[\mathrm{Ca}]_\text{in})$.

**State vector** (`MOLECULE_NAMES`, lines 105‚Äď199, `N_SPECIES = 58`): cytosolic &
DTS free Ca¬≤‚Āļ (`CA2_CYT[c]`, `CA2_DTS[dts]`), IP‚āÉ, the IP‚āÉR inactivation gate
`IP3R_h`, SERCA's 6 enzyme states, PMCA's basal + CaM-coupled states, STIM1
(free/Ca/dimer), Orai1, CaM (free/Ca‚āā/Ca‚āĄ), all receptor pools (P2X1, P2Y1,
PAR1, PAR4, P2Y12, TP), Gq, PLCő≤ (inactive/active/phosphorylated), PIP‚āā, DAG,
PKC (inactive/active), cAMP, VASP, mitochondrial Ca¬≤‚Āļ, and the DTS luminal
buffers (CALR, HSP90B1, BiP, CREC, GSN).

---

## A. Agonist inputs and receptors

### A.1 Agonist forcing time-courses

All three exogenous agonists share a rise-then-decay form with $t_\text{eff} = t -
\text{delay}$ (returns REST for $t_\text{eff}\le0$):

$$\text{value}(t) = \text{REST} + (\text{PEAK}-\text{REST})\,
\underbrace{(1-e^{-t_\text{eff}/\tau_\text{rise}})}_{\text{rise}}\;
\underbrace{e^{-\max(0,\,t_\text{eff}-t_\text{peak})/\tau_\text{decay}}}_{\text{decay}}$$

| Agonist | fn / lines | REST | PEAK | $\tau_\text{rise}$ | $t_\text{peak}$ | $\tau_\text{decay}$ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Thrombin (nM) | `thrombin_nM` 707‚Äď717 | 0 | 1.0 | 0.5 s | 5 s | 120 s |
| ADP (¬ĶM) | `adp_uM` 720‚Äď730 | 0 | 10.0 | 1.0 s | 5 s | 30 s |
| ATP_ex (¬ĶM) | `atp_ex_forcing_uM` 485‚Äď499 | 0 | 10.0 | 0.5 s | 1 s | 30 s |

`TOML [agonists.*]` 161‚Äď187. Per-call `peak_*=None` reads the module default;
`peak=0` holds REST. `RunConfig` overrides via `thrombin_peak_nM` / `adp_peak_uM`
/ `atp_ex_peak_uM` / `agonist_delay_s` (lines 931‚Äď934). The **agonist delay** is
how the demo experiments impose a settle: e.g. `agonist_delay_s = 300` holds all
agonists at REST for 300 s before stimulation.

### A.2 P2X1 ‚ÄĒ ATP-gated ionotropic Ca¬≤‚Āļ channel

**Role.** Fast plasma-membrane ATP-gated cation channel; the rapid, +Ca¬≤‚Āļ-ex‚Äďspecific
entry pathway. **Three-state** closed/open/desensitised cycle (lines 1290‚Äď1300):

$$\dot C = -v_\text{act}+v_\text{close}+v_\text{rec},\quad
\dot O = v_\text{act}-v_\text{close}-v_\text{des},\quad
\dot D = v_\text{des}-v_\text{rec}$$
$$v_\text{act}=k_\text{act}\,C\,[\mathrm{ATP}]_\text{ex},\;
v_\text{close}=k_\text{close}\,O,\;
v_\text{des}=k_\text{des}\,O,\;
v_\text{rec}=k_\text{rec}\,D$$

Ca¬≤‚Āļ entry (only when $\text{ca\_ex}>0$, lines 1266‚Äď1269):
$J_\text{P2X1} = -\gamma_\text{P2X1}\,O\,(V_{PM}-E_{Ca,PM})\,N_A/zF \to$ `CA2_CYT[c]`.

| Constant | Value | Units | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| `k_act` | 30.0 | ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | Mahaut-Smith 2000/2004 |
| `k_close` | 5.0 | s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | ‚ÄĒ |
| `k_des` | 10.0 | s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą100 ms) | Mahaut-Smith |
| `k_rec` | 0.03 | s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą30 s) | Mahaut-Smith |
| `n_total` | 1000 | trimers | ‚ÄĒ |
| `gamma_s` | $1.3\times10^{-15}$ | A/V (1.3 fS) | calibrated |

`TOML [p2x1.kinetics]/[p2x1.channel]` 226‚Äď242. **Terminal entry** into the ODE.

### A.3 P2Y1 ‚Üí Gq (ADP, reversible) + PKC desensitisation

**Role.** Gq-coupled ADP receptor; reversible binding, PKC-desensitisable. ADP
drive includes the autocrine secreted contribution (lines 1358‚Äď1360):
$\text{adp\_um\_now} = \text{adp\_uM}(t) + \text{secreted\_adp\_count}\cdot
\texttt{\_UM\_PER\_COUNT\_EX}\cdot \texttt{autocrine\_adp\_gain}$.

$$\dot{R_i}=-v_\text{on}+v_\text{off}+v_\text{res},\quad
\dot{R_a}=v_\text{on}-v_\text{off}-v_\text{des},\quad
\dot{R_\text{des}}=v_\text{des}-v_\text{res}$$
$$v_\text{on}=k_\text{on}R_i[\mathrm{ADP}],\;v_\text{off}=k_\text{off}R_a,\;
v_\text{des}=k_\text{des}\,\texttt{k\_des\_scale}\,\mathrm{PKC}_a R_a,\;
v_\text{res}=k_\text{res}R_\text{des}$$

| Constant | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `k_on` | 1.0 ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | model choice (~150 copies) |
| `k_off` | 0.5 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | model choice |
| `k_des` (desens.) | $2.0\times10^{-5}$ count‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | Mundell 2006, Nicholas 2023 |
| `k_res` (resens.) | 0.01 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą100 s) | ‚ÄĒ |

`TOML [gpcr.p2y1]` 349‚Äď350, `[pkc.p2y1_desens]` 772‚Äď773. $R_a$ joins
`total_active_R` driving Gq. **Gq feedback.**

### A.4 PAR1 + PAR4 ‚ÄĒ thrombin protease-activated receptors

**Role.** Irreversible thrombin cleavage; PAR1 fast/high-affinity, PAR4 ~10√ó
slower/sustained; one-way internalisation (lines 1385‚Äď1397):

$$\dot{R_i}=-v_\text{cleave},\quad \dot{R_a}=v_\text{cleave}-v_\text{int},\quad
\dot{R_\text{int}}=v_\text{int};\qquad
v_\text{cleave}=k_\text{cleave}R_i[\mathrm{thr}],\; v_\text{int}=k_\text{int}R_a$$

| Receptor | `k_cleave` | `k_internalize` | copies | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PAR1 | 2.0 nM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | 0.02 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą50 s) | ~2500 | Vu 1991, Hammes & Coughlin 1999 |
| PAR4 | 0.2 nM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | 0.005 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą200 s) | ~500 | Kahn 1998, Covic 2000 |

`TOML [gpcr.par1]/[gpcr.par4]` 366‚Äď379. Both active pools join `total_active_R`.
**Gq feedback.**

### A.5 P2Y12 ‚Üí Gi ‚Üí cAMP ‚Üí PKA (the inhibitory arm)

**Role.** Gi-coupled ADP receptor. Lowers cAMP ‚Üí lowers PKA ‚Üí **dis-inhibits** the
PKA brake on IP‚āÉR, integrin and secretion. `p2y12_block` ‚ąą [0,1] is the
clopidogrel/cangrelor knob. Implemented **inside the ODE** (lines 1425‚Äď1454).

Receptor: $v_\text{on}=k_\text{on}R_i\,\text{adp}\,\text{avail}$,
$\text{avail}=1-\text{clamp}_{[0,1]}(\texttt{p2y12\_block})$; $v_\text{off}=k_\text{off}R_a$.

cAMP node:
$$\text{p2y12\_frac}=\min(1,R_a/N_{P2Y12}),\quad
\text{gs\_stim}=\frac{\text{pgi2\_nM}}{\text{pgi2\_nM}+K_\text{pgi2}}$$
$$v_\text{AC}=V_\text{AC,BASAL}(1+\text{ac\_gs\_max}\cdot\text{gs\_stim}+\text{forskolin})
\max(0,\,1-i_\text{gi,max}\,\text{p2y12\_frac})$$
$$v_\text{PDE}=k_\text{pde}\,\max(0,1-\texttt{pde3\_block})\,[\mathrm{cAMP}],\qquad
\frac{d[\mathrm{cAMP}]}{dt}=v_\text{AC}-v_\text{PDE}$$

with $V_\text{AC,BASAL}=k_\text{pde}\cdot\text{CAMP\_REST\_COUNT}$ so cAMP rests
exactly at basal. PKA activity (Hill of cAMP, lines 642‚Äď648):
$\text{pka\_frac}=\dfrac{[\mathrm{cAMP}]^n}{k_\text{pka}^n+[\mathrm{cAMP}]^n}$.
The **PKA brake** (lines 664‚Äď677), normalised to 1.0 at rest:

$$b = \text{clamp}\big(1+\text{gain}\,(\text{PKA\_REST\_FRAC}-\text{pka\_frac}),\,
b_\text{min},\,b_\text{max}\big)$$

| Constant | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| P2Y12 `n_total` | 425 | Gachet 2012 / Ohlmann 2011 |
| `k_on` / `k_off` | 1.0 ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ / 0.5 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | model choice |
| `camp_rest_uM` | 1.0 ¬ĶM | Blood 2014 |
| `k_pde` | 0.1 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | model choice |
| `i_gi_max` | 0.8 | calibrated |
| `ac_gs_max`, `K_pgi2_nM` | 6.0, 5.0 nM | calibrated |
| `k_pka_uM`, `n_pka` | 1.0 ¬ĶM, 2 | model choice |
| IP‚āÉR brake `gain`,`b_min`,`b_max` | 1.0, 0.2, 2.0 | model choice |
| integrin / secretion brake `gain`,`b_max` | 2.0, 3.0 | model choice |
| VASP `n_total`,`k_phos`,`k_dephos` | 50000, 0.1, 0.06 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | Burkhart 2012 |

`TOML [gpcr.p2y12]` 427‚Äď429, `[inhibitory.camp/pka/vasp]` 456‚Äď496. The brake
multiplies the IP‚āÉR open probability **inside** the ODE (`b_ip3r`, line 1054);
the integrin and secretion brakes are applied in their own processes. VASP
phosphorylation is a pure readout: $\dfrac{d[\mathrm{VASP\_phos}]}{dt}=k_\text{phos}
\,\text{pka\_frac}\,\mathrm{VASP}_\text{free}-k_\text{dephos}\,\mathrm{VASP}_\text{phos}$.
cAMP-raising drug inputs (all RunConfig, default 0): `pgi2_nM` (PGI‚āā/iloprost),
`forskolin`, `pde3_block` (cilostazol). **Gi feedback.**

### A.6 TP (TXA‚āā receptor) ‚Üí Gq

**Role.** Gq-coupled thromboxane receptor; reversible and **catalytic** (binding
does not deplete TXA‚āā). Autocrine TXA‚āā from the synthesis process (lines 1403‚Äď1407):
$\text{txa2\_um\_now}=\text{secreted\_txa2\_count}\cdot\texttt{\_UM\_PER\_COUNT\_EX}
\cdot\texttt{autocrine\_txa2\_gain}$.

$$\dot{R_i}=-v_\text{on}+v_\text{off},\;\dot{R_a}=v_\text{on}-v_\text{off};\quad
v_\text{on}=k_\text{on}R_i[\mathrm{TXA2}],\;v_\text{off}=k_\text{off}R_a$$

`k_on=1.0` ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, `k_off=0.5` s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, ~1000 copies (`TOML [gpcr.tp]` 390‚Äď391, model
choice). $R_a$ joins `total_active_R`. **Gq feedback** (closed by ¬ßG.2).

---

## B. G-protein cascade: GőĪq ‚Üí PLCő≤

**Role.** A shared GőĪq pool driven by all active Gq receptors; activates PLCő≤,
which PKC can phosphorylate out of the activatable pool (lines 1411‚Äď1415, 1309‚Äď1334).

$$\text{total\_active\_R}=P2Y1_a+PAR1_a+PAR4_a+TP_a$$
$$\dot{Gq_a}=(k_\text{act/R}\,\text{total\_active\_R}+k_\text{basal})\,Gq_i-k_\text{rgs}Gq_a,\quad
\text{gq\_um}=\tfrac{Gq_a}{N_{GQ}}\cdot5.0$$

PLCő≤ activation + PKC phospho-brake:
$$\dot{PLCb_i}=-v_\text{act}+v_\text{inact}-v_\text{phos}+v_\text{dephos},\;
\dot{PLCb_a}=v_\text{act}-v_\text{inact},\;
\dot{PLCb_p}=v_\text{phos}-v_\text{dephos}$$
$$v_\text{act}=k_\text{act}PLCb_i\,\text{gq\_um},\;v_\text{inact}=k_\text{inact}PLCb_a,\;
v_\text{phos}=k_\text{phos}\,\texttt{k\_plcb\_phos\_scale}\,\mathrm{PKC}_a PLCb_i,\;
v_\text{dephos}=k_\text{dephos}PLCb_p$$

| Constant | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `k_act_per_R` | 0.001 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļcount‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | calibrated |
| `k_rgs` | 0.033 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą30 s) | ‚ÄĒ |
| `k_basal` | $6.7\times10^{-4}$ s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | calibrated |
| `N_GQ_TOTAL` | 5000 | Mazet 2020 |
| PLCő≤ `k_act`,`k_inact` | 0.5 ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, 0.3 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | ‚ÄĒ |
| PLCő≤-phos `k_phos`,`k_dephos` | $3.0\times10^{-5}$ count‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, 0.02 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | Purvis 2008 |

`TOML [gpcr.gq]/[gpcr.gq_pool]` 404‚Äď413, `[pi_cycle.plcb]` 511‚Äď512,
`[pkc.plcb_phos]` 791‚Äď792.

---

## C. PI cycle: PIP‚āā ‚Üí IP‚āÉ + DAG

**Role.** Endogenous IP‚āÉ/DAG production and turnover (lines 1313‚Äď1337).

$$\dot{\mathrm{PIP2}}=-v_\text{cat}+v_\text{resynth},\quad
\dot{\mathrm{IP3}}=v_\text{cat}-v_\text{ip3,deg},\quad
\dot{\mathrm{DAG}}=v_\text{cat}-v_\text{dag,deg}$$
$$v_\text{cat}=k_\text{cat}\,PLCb_a\,\mathrm{PIP2},\;v_\text{resynth}=k_\text{resynth},\;
v_\text{ip3,deg}=k_\text{ip3,deg}\,\mathrm{IP3},\;v_\text{dag,deg}=k_\text{dag,deg}\,\mathrm{DAG}$$

| Constant | Value | Meaning / source |
|---|---|---|
| `k_cat` | $2.26\times10^{-8}$ count‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | PIP‚āā hydrolysis |
| `k_resynth` | 3.62 PIP‚āā/s | zero-order resynthesis |
| `k_ip3_deg` | 0.02 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą50 s) | 5-phosphatase + 3-kinase, Dolan Fig. S2 |
| `k_dag_deg` | 0.05 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą20 s) | DAG kinase |

`TOML [pi_cycle.plcb]` 513, `[pi_cycle.metabolism]` 519‚Äď521. Resting IP‚āÉ ‚Čą 0.05 ¬ĶM
(`[resting]` 27).

---

## D. PKC (őĪ/ő≤/őī) ‚ÄĒ the activation hub

**Role.** DAG + Ca¬≤‚Āļ coincidence detector. In the ODE it activates from the DAG
branch with a Hill Ca¬≤‚Āļ co-factor (lines 1345‚Äď1352):

$$f_{Ca}=f_\text{floor}+(1-f_\text{floor})\frac{Ca^{n}}{K_{Ca}^{n}+Ca^{n}},\quad
\dot{PKC_a}=k_\text{act}\,PKC_i\,\text{dag\_uM}\,f_{Ca}-k_\text{inact}PKC_a$$

`k_act=0.7` ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, `k_inact=0.05` s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą20 s), `K_Ca_PKC=0.7` ¬ĶM, `n_Ca_PKC=2`,
`f_ca_floor=0` (`TOML [pkc.kinetics]` 751‚Äď755; shape after Purvis 2008).
$PKC_a$ drives P2Y1 desensitisation (¬ßA.3), PLCő≤ phosphorylation (¬ßB), and ‚ÄĒ via
the **shared coincidence gate** below ‚ÄĒ all three terminal outputs.

**The shared `pkc_ca_gate`** (`calcium_signalling.py:82‚Äď97`), used identically by
GranuleSecretion, ThromboxaneSynthesis and IntegrinActivation:

$$\text{gate}=\underbrace{\frac{\max(\mathrm{PKC}^*_{\mu M}-\text{floor},0)}
{\max(\mathrm{PKC}^*_{\mu M}-\text{floor},0)+K_\text{pkc}}}_{\text{pkc\_term}}
\cdot\underbrace{\frac{Ca^{n_{ca}}}{Ca^{n_{ca}}+K_{ca}^{n_{ca}}}}_{\text{ca\_term}}
\;\in[0,1)$$

All three consumers use the same constants: `PKC_floor_uM=0.05`, `K_pkc_uM=0.30`,
`K_ca_uM=0.40`, `n_ca=2` (model choices). The floor makes the gate **exactly 0 at
rest** ‚ÄĒ the resting-quiescence invariant that keeps secretion, thromboxane and
integrin activation at zero in an unstimulated platelet.

---

## E. Calcium fluxes ‚ÄĒ store and plasma membrane

### E.1 IP‚āÉR ‚ÄĒ Li-Rinzel reduction of De Young‚ÄďKeizer

**Role.** IP‚āÉ- and Ca¬≤‚Āļ-gated DTS‚Üícytosol release channel (lines 1044‚Äď1075).
With $h = \mathrm{IP3R\_h}/N_\text{IP3R}$:

$$m_\infty=\frac{\mathrm{IP3}}{\mathrm{IP3}+d_1}\cdot\frac{Ca_\text{cyt}}{Ca_\text{cyt}+d_5},\quad
P_o=m_\infty^4\,h\,b_\text{ip3r},\quad
\frac{dh}{dt}=a_2\big(d_2-(Ca_\text{cyt}+d_2)h\big)$$
$$J_\text{IP3R}=-\gamma_\text{IP3R}N_\text{IP3R}P_o(V_{IM}-E_{Ca,IM})\tfrac{N_A}{zF}\cdot
\text{mito\_coupling\_factor}\;\to\;+\mathrm{CA2\_CYT},\,-\mathrm{CA2\_DTS}$$

$b_\text{ip3r}$ is the PKA brake (¬ßA.5); `mito_coupling_factor` is the MCU relief
(¬ßG.3). Constants: `d1=0.13`, `d2=1.049`, `d5=0.08234` ¬ĶM, `a2=0.2` ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ
(`TOML [ip3r.k_dyk]` 66‚Äď69); `n_total=1328` (Dolan 2014 Table S1),
`gamma_s=0.135` pS (recalibrated 2026-06-16 for $V_{IM}=0$) (`[ip3r.channel]` 95‚Äď96).
Refs: De Young & Keizer 1992, Li & Rinzel 1994.

### E.2 SERCA ‚ÄĒ E1/E2 enzymatic cycle

**Role.** ATP-driven cytosol‚ÜíDTS pump, 2 Ca¬≤‚Āļ/turnover; 6 enzyme states
(lines 1078‚Äď1096):

$$v_\text{bind}=k_{bf}\,se1\,Ca_\text{cyt}^2-k_{br}\,se1c,\quad
v_\text{release}=k_{rf}\,se2pc-k_{rr}\,se2p\,Ca_\text{dts}^2$$
$$\dot{\mathrm{CA2\_CYT}}\mathrel{-}=2v_\text{bind},\qquad
\dot{\mathrm{CA2\_DTS}}\mathrel{+}=2v_\text{release}$$

(plus shuttle/phos/conf/dephos steps closing the cycle). Forward/reverse rates
`k_shuttle=600/600`, `k_bind=210/10`, `k_phos=700/5`, `k_conf=600/50`,
`k_release=1000/0.004`, `k_dephos=500/1` (s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ or ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬≤s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ; `TOML [serca.cycle]`
125‚Äď136; Purvis 2008 Table 1 / Dode 2002 isoform 3b). **Calibration-coupled with
$\gamma_\text{IP3R}$** ‚ÄĒ changing one requires re-deriving the other.

### E.3 PMCA ‚ÄĒ 5-state CaM-coupled extrusion

**Role.** Plasma-membrane cytosol‚Üíextracellular pump; a basal path and a
Ca‚āĄ¬∑CaM-activated path with ~5√ó higher $k_\text{cat}$ (lines 1158‚Äď1210).

$$\text{basal: }\;v_\text{bind}=k_\text{on}\,\mathrm{PMCA}\,Ca_\text{cyt}-k_\text{off}\,\mathrm{PMCA\_Ca},\quad
v_\text{cat}=k_\text{cat}\,\texttt{pmca\_kcat\_scale}\,\mathrm{PMCA\_Ca}$$

Basal `k_on=10` ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, `k_off=50` s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, `k_cat=5.5` s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (`TOML [pmca.basal]`
194‚Äď196). CaM-activated steps 8‚Äď12: `k8=0.2`, `k8r=8e-4`, `k9=50`, `k9r=10`,
`k10=30`, `k11=10`, `k11r=7.332e-4`, `k12=1.0` (`[pmca.cam_activated]` 720‚Äď727;
Caride 2007 Table 3). Removes Ca¬≤‚Āļ from `CA2_CYT[c]`.

### E.4 NCX ‚ÄĒ Na‚Āļ/Ca¬≤‚Āļ exchanger

**Role.** Forward-mode Ca¬≤‚Āļ extrusion (3 Na‚Āļ:1 Ca¬≤‚Āļ), Ca¬≤‚Āļ-activated; runs in both
¬ĪCa¬≤‚Āļ-ex conditions (lines 1284‚Äď1288):

$$g_\text{act}=\frac{(Ca_\text{cyt}/K_a)^h}{1+(Ca_\text{cyt}/K_a)^h},\quad
v_\text{ncx}=V_\text{max}\,g_\text{act}\,\frac{Ca_\text{cyt}}{K_m+Ca_\text{cyt}}\;\to\;-\mathrm{CA2\_CYT}$$

`V_max=5000` ions/s (calibration anchor), `K_m=5.0` ¬ĶM, `K_a=0.2` ¬ĶM, `h=4`
(`TOML [ncx.kinetics]` 318‚Äď321; Blaustein & Lederer 1999, Roberts 2012).

### E.5 Basal plasma-membrane Ca¬≤‚Āļ leak

Constant cytosolic influx balancing PMCA at rest, gated on extracellular Ca¬≤‚Āļ
(line 1259): $\dot{\mathrm{CA2\_CYT}}\mathrel{+}= J_\text{PM,leak}$, `ions_s=75.0`
(`TOML [pm.leak]` 54; calibrated against the PM balance at 100 nM cyt / 250 ¬ĶM DTS).

---

## F. SOCE ‚ÄĒ store-operated Ca¬≤‚Āļ entry (STIM1 / Orai1)

**Role.** Store depletion ‚Üí STIM1 dimerisation ‚Üí Orai1 opening ‚Üí Ca¬≤‚Āļ entry; the
sustained-plateau pathway. Gated on extracellular Ca¬≤‚Āļ (Dolan 2014).

STIM1 cycle (lines 1213‚Äď1223): Ca¬≤‚Āļ unbinding from the luminal STIM1 EF-hand and
free-monomer dimerisation,
$v_\text{release}=k_{rf}\,st_{ca}-k_{rr}\,st_\text{free}Ca_\text{dts}$,
$v_\text{dim}=k_{df}\,st_\text{free}^2-k_{dr}\,st_\text{dim}$. Puncta drive (Dolan
eq. 2, lines 1227‚Äď1235): $q_p=\alpha\frac{Ca_\text{cyt}^n}{KM^n+Ca_\text{cyt}^n}+\text{baseline}$,
$\text{stim2\_p}=q_p\,st_\text{dim}$. Orai1 open fraction is the MWC equilibrium
solved numerically (`_mwc_open_fraction`, lines 849‚Äď910):

$$P_o=\frac{\sum_i os_i}{\sum_i(cs_i+os_i)},\quad
cs_i=\binom{4}{i}a^{i(i-1)/2}x^i,\; os_i=cs_i\,L\,f^i,\; x=K_a\,s_f$$

solved by damped fixed-point iteration. SOC flux (only if $\text{ca\_ex}>0$,
lines 1246‚Äď1255): $J_\text{SOCE}=-\gamma_\text{SOC}\,n_\text{orai}\,P_o\,(V_{PM}-E_{Ca,PM})\,N_A/zF$.

| Constant | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| STIM `k_release_f/r` | 0.1 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ / $3.475\times10^{-3}$ ¬ĶM‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | ‚ÄĒ |
| STIM `k_dim_f/r` | $5.73\times10^{-5}$ count‚ĀĽ¬Ļs‚ĀĽ¬Ļ / 1.0 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | ‚ÄĒ |
| MWC `L`,`Ka`,`f`,`a` | $10^{-4}$, 2.0, 14.2, 0.5 | Hoover & Lewis 2011 |
| puncta `alpha`,`KM`,`n`,`baseline` | 0.2, 0.5 ¬ĶM, 4.0, 0.01 | Dolan 2014 |
| Orai `gamma_s` | 0.3 fS | calibrated |

`TOML [soce.stim/mwc/puncta/orai]` 39, 256‚Äď287. `Orai1[pl]` is read-only (channel
count, not a differentiated state). **The validation target** is Dolan & Diamond
2014 Fig. 4 (Ca¬≤‚Āļ transients with vs without extracellular Ca¬≤‚Āļ): SOCE is the
differentiating mechanism.

---

## G. Mitochondria, buffers, and the autocrine outputs

### G.1 MCU + NCLX (+ IP‚āÉR-relief coupling, #76)

**Role.** Mitochondrial uniporter ‚ÄĒ fast cooperative uptake, capacity-bounded
(#76 Part 1) ‚ÄĒ plus NCLX slow linear efflux; MCU uptake also **relieves IP‚āÉR
inactivation** (#76 Part 2) (lines 1459‚Äď1474, 817‚Äď839).

$$\text{mito\_fill}=\max\!\Big(0,1-\frac{\mathrm{Ca}_\text{mito}}{C_\text{max}}\Big),\quad
v_\text{MCU}=V_\text{max}\,\texttt{mcu\_vmax\_scale}\,\frac{Ca_\text{cyt}^{n}}{K_\text{MCU}^{n}+Ca_\text{cyt}^{n}}\,\text{mito\_fill}$$
$$v_\text{NCLX}=k_\text{NCLX}\,\mathrm{Ca}_\text{mito},\qquad
\dot{\mathrm{CA2\_CYT}}\mathrel{+}=-v_\text{MCU}+v_\text{NCLX},\;
\dot{\mathrm{CA2\_MITO}}\mathrel{+}=v_\text{MCU}-v_\text{NCLX}$$

IP‚āÉR relief (`ip3r_relief_factor`, multiplies $J_\text{IP3R}$ only):
$\text{act}=\frac{Ca^{n_\text{act}}}{Ca^{n_\text{act}}+Ka_\text{act}^{n_\text{act}}}$,
$\text{loss}=\text{gain}\cdot\text{coupling}\cdot\max(0,1-\texttt{mcu\_vmax\_scale})\cdot\text{act}$,
$\text{factor}=\max(0,1-\text{loss})$. At wild-type ($\texttt{mcu\_vmax\_scale}=1$),
factor = 1.

`V_max_MCU=50000` ions/s, `K_MCU=1.0` ¬ĶM, `n_MCU=4`, `k_NCLX=0.005` s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ=200 s),
`C_max=100000` ions (`TOML [mito.kinetics]` 532‚Äď536); `coupling_strength=0.95`,
`Ka_act=0.25` ¬ĶM, `n_act=4` (`[mito.bioenergetic]` 559‚Äď561); `gain =
config.mito_coupling_gain`. Refs: Ghatge 2026, Ajanel 2025. (See
`reports/experiments/3-mcu-knockout.qmd` for the KO phenotype.)

### G.2 Buffers ‚ÄĒ CaM and the DTS luminal proteins

**CaM** (cytosolic, two-lobe cooperative; lines 1100‚Äď1108):
$v_1=k_6\,\mathrm{CaM_{free}}Ca^2-k_{6r}\mathrm{Ca_2CaM}$,
$v_2=k_7\,\mathrm{Ca_2CaM}\,Ca^2-k_{7r}\mathrm{Ca_4CaM}$;
$\dot{\mathrm{CA2\_CYT}}\mathrel{+}=-2v_1-2v_2$. Ca‚āĄ¬∑CaM activates PMCA (¬ßE.3).
`k6=2.669`, `k6r=2.682`, `k7=170.4`, `k7r=1.551` (`TOML [cam.binding]` 204‚Äď207;
Caride 2007 steps 6‚Äď7).

**DTS luminal buffers** (1:1 mass-action $v=k_\text{on}\,\text{free}\,Ca-k_\text{off}\,\text{bound}$,
drawing from `CA2_DTS`; GSN draws from `CA2_CYT`): CALR (+ phosphorylated CALR_P),
HSP90B1 (medium/low affinity), BiP, CREC, GSN. Pool counts: GSN 1.4M, CALR 508k
(+ CALR_P 20k), HSP90B1 10k√ó(4 medium / 11 low), BiP 50k, CREC 15k√ó4. Constants
in `TOML [buffers.*]` 581‚Äď700.

### G.3 Granule secretion (+ autocrine ADP loop)

**Role.** PKC√óCa¬≤‚Āļ-gated dense- and őĪ-granule cargo release; closes the autocrine
ADP ‚Üí P2Y1 loop. Files: `granule_secretion.py`. First-order release per pool,
gated and PKA-braked (lines 104‚Äď125):

$$\text{released}_i=\big\lfloor \text{src}_i\,(1-e^{-k_{sec,i}\,\text{gate}\,b_\text{PKA}\,\Delta t})\big\rfloor$$

Cargo map: `ADP[dg]‚ÜíADP[e]`, `5HT[dg]‚Üí5HT[e]` (dense), `FGA[ag]‚ÜíFGA[e]`,
`SELP[ag]‚ÜíSELP_surface[pl]` (őĪ). Ecto-NTPDase clearance:
$\text{cleared}=\lfloor\mathrm{ADP[e]}(1-e^{-k_\text{ntpdase}\Delta t})\rfloor$,
ADP[e]‚ÜíAMP[e]. Constants: `k_sec={dense:0.10, alpha:0.05}` s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, `k_ntpdase=0.05`
s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ŌĄ‚Čą20 s), gate as ¬ßD, brake `gain=2.0`/`b_max=3.0`.

**Autocrine ADP (Slice 2)** is consumed in the ODE (¬ßA.3): secreted ADP[e] ‚Üí
pericellular ¬ĶM via `V_EX_L=6.64e-14` L (calibration: full dense-granule release
‚Čą 10 ¬ĶM). Release itself is a **terminal output**; the ADP loop is **Gq (and Gi)
feedback**.

### G.4 Thromboxane synthesis (+ autocrine TP loop)

**Role.** Lumped cPLA‚āā‚ÜíCOX-1‚ÜíTXA‚āā-synthase production, Ca¬≤‚Āļ√óPKC-gated, with the
aspirin knob; TXA‚āā‚ÜíTXB‚āā decay. File: `thromboxane_synthesis.py` (lines 70‚Äď89):

$$\text{produced}=\text{round}(k_\text{prod}\,\texttt{cox1\_factor}\,\text{gate}\,\Delta t),\quad
\text{decayed}=\lfloor\mathrm{TXA2[e]}(1-e^{-k_\text{decay}\Delta t})\rfloor$$

`TXA2[e] += produced ‚ąí decayed`, `TXB2[e] += decayed`. `k_prod=2500` molecules/s
at full gate, `k_decay=0.0231` s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (t¬Ĺ‚Čą30 s), gate as ¬ßD. `cox1_factor` (RunConfig,
default 1; **0 = aspirin** ‚ÄĒ abolishes TXA‚āā). Production is a **terminal output**;
the TP‚ÜíGq loop (¬ßA.6) is **Gq feedback**, implemented in the ODE.

### G.5 őĪIIbő≤3 inside-out activation (PAC-1 readout) + Akt/Rap1b arm

**Role.** 2-state resting‚áĆactive őĪIIbő≤3 switch; active fraction = the per-cell
PAC-1 readout. Driven by **Rap1b-GTP**, sustained by P2Y12‚ÜíAkt, gated and
PKA-braked. File: `integrin_activation.py`.

PI3K/Akt ‚Üí Rap1b (pure helper `akt_rap_step`, lines 35‚Äď62; Euler-exact 1 s step),
with $\text{p2y12\_frac}=\mathrm{P2Y12\_active}/N_{P2Y12}$:

$$\text{akt}_{next}=\text{akt\_ss}+(\text{akt}-\text{akt\_ss})e^{-k_\text{tot}\Delta t},\;
\text{akt\_ss}=\frac{k_\text{akt,on}\,\text{p2y12\_frac}}{k_\text{akt,on}\,\text{p2y12\_frac}+k_\text{akt,off}}$$
$$b_\text{gap}=k_\text{rap,gap}\max(0,1-f_\text{akt,gap}\,\text{akt}_{next}),\quad
a_\text{form}=k_\text{rap,form}\,\text{gate}$$
$$\mathrm{Rap1b}_{next}=\text{rap\_ss}+(\mathrm{Rap1b}-\text{rap\_ss})e^{-(a_\text{form}+b_\text{gap})\Delta t},\;
\text{rap\_ss}=\frac{a_\text{form}}{a_\text{form}+b_\text{gap}}$$

Akt dis-inhibits the Rasa3 GAP, so Rap1b-GTP stays high while P2Y12 is driven.
The conformational switch (lines 157‚Äď177):

$$\text{activated}=\big\lfloor\mathrm{resting}\,(1-e^{-k_\text{act}\,\text{act\_scale}\,\text{rap1b\_scale}\,\mathrm{Rap1b\_GTP}\,b_\text{PKA}\,\Delta t})\big\rfloor,\;
\text{reverted}=\lfloor\mathrm{active}\,(1-e^{-k_\text{inact}\Delta t})\rfloor$$

| Constant | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `k_act` / `k_inact` | 0.05 / 0.005 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | model choice |
| `k_rap_form` / `k_rap_gap` | 0.30 / 0.08 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | #73, model choice (Zou 2022, Stolla 2011) |
| `f_akt_gap` | 0.90 | model choice |
| `k_akt_on` / `k_akt_off` | 0.04 / 0.02 s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ | model choice |
| `N_P2Y12_TOTAL` | 425 | Gachet 2012 |
| brake `gain` / `b_max` | 2.0 / 3.0 | model choice |

KO knobs: `integrin_act_scale` (0 = őĪIIbő≤3 antagonist / Glanzmann ‚ÄĒ receptor KO),
`rap1b_scale` (0 = Rap1b/CalDAG-GEFI loss ‚ÄĒ pathway KO). **Terminal output** ‚ÄĒ the
calcium ODE is untouched (Dolan goldens byte-identical), though it *reads* the
P2Y12 and cAMP states the ODE writes.

---

## Feedback loops & terminal outputs ‚ÄĒ summary

| Pathway / arm | Couples to Ca¬≤‚Āļ ODE? |
|---|---|
| P2X1, P2Y1, PAR1, PAR4, TP, Gq ‚Üí PLCő≤ ‚Üí PI cycle ‚Üí IP‚āÉR | Yes (the core Gq cascade) |
| P2Y12 ‚Üí Gi ‚Üí cAMP ‚Üí PKA ‚Üí IP‚āÉR brake | **Gi feedback** (in ODE) |
| PKC ‚Üí P2Y1 desensitisation; PKC ‚Üí PLCő≤ phosphorylation | **Gq brakes** (in ODE) |
| Granule secretion ‚ÄĒ autocrine **ADP** ‚Üí P2Y1/P2Y12 | **Gq/Gi feedback** |
| Thromboxane ‚ÄĒ autocrine **TXA‚āā** ‚Üí TP ‚Üí Gq | **Gq feedback** |
| Granule release / thromboxane production / integrin activation | **Terminal outputs** (no ODE coupling) |

PKC is the hub of **four feedback loops** (P2Y1 desensitisation, PLCő≤
phosphorylation, autocrine ADP, autocrine TXA‚āā) **plus three terminal outputs**
(secretion, thromboxane, integrin).

## RunConfig knobs that change the biology

| Field | Effect |
|---|---|
| `ca_ex_mM` | extracellular Ca¬≤‚Āļ; 0 gates off SOCE / PM-leak / P2X1 (Dolan EDTA) |
| `thrombin/adp/atp_ex_peak_*`, `agonist_delay_s` | agonist time-courses & settle |
| `autocrine_adp_gain`, `autocrine_txa2_gain` | open/close the two autocrine loops |
| `cox1_factor` | aspirin (0 = COX-1 knockout) |
| `p2y12_block` | clopidogrel/cangrelor (1 = full P2Y12 block) |
| `pgi2_nM`, `forskolin`, `pde3_block` | cAMP-raising arms (PGI‚āā, forskolin, cilostazol) |
| `mcu_vmax_scale`, `mito_coupling_gain` | MCU uptake KO + IP‚āÉR-relief toggle |
| `pmca_kcat_scale`, `k_des_scale`, `k_plcb_phos_scale` | per-mechanism scales |
| `integrin_act_scale`, `rap1b_scale` | integrin receptor KO / Rap1b pathway KO |

## Sources

Receptor copy numbers (P2Y12 425; PAR1 ~2500; PAR4 ~500; VASP 50000), the IP‚āÉR
count (1328) and the SOCE framework are from measured/primary literature
(Gachet 2012, Ohlmann 2011, Burkhart 2012, Dolan & Diamond 2014, Hoover & Lewis
2011). The IP‚āÉR, SERCA, PMCA, CaM and MWC *functional forms* are from De Young &
Keizer 1992 / Li & Rinzel 1994, Dode 2002 / Purvis 2008, Caride 2007 and Hoover &
Lewis 2011 respectively. Most individual rate constants are **calibration or
model choices** (so noted above); the calcium pathway is validated against Dolan
& Diamond 2014 Fig. 4 (the "5/5" peak / SOCE-differential criteria). Full
provenance with inline citations is in `reports/design/kinetics-v0.6-review.qmd`
(rendered from the TOML) and `reports/archive/calcium-dynamics-design.md`.

## How this document was produced

**This document was generated by an AI coding assistant (Claude Code), by reading
the model source directly.** Every equation and rate constant above was extracted
from the implementation ‚ÄĒ `reconstruction/platelet/dataclasses/process/calcium_signalling.py`
(the `_ode_rhs` right-hand side and its helpers), `reports/params/calcium-v0.6.toml`
(the `_KINETICS` rate-constant table), and the three downstream process files
(`granule_secretion.py`, `thromboxane_synthesis.py`, `integrin_activation.py`) ‚ÄĒ
with line-number citations to the code as of git commit `4fe847b9`. No equation or
numeric value was drawn from the assistant's own prior knowledge; the source code
and TOML are the sole authority.

This has two consequences worth stating plainly. First, the document is a
**faithful transcription of what the code does**, not an independent statement of
what the biology *should* be ‚ÄĒ if the implementation contains an error, this
reference reproduces it. Second, AI extraction over ~1500 lines can mis-attribute a
constant or paraphrase an equation; **treat the cited source line as ground truth
and this document as a navigational aid.** The literature citations (paper,
author, year) are reproduced from comments in the code/TOML and have not been
independently re-verified against the original publications here.
